title: READABILITY: Clarify pre-auth frame handling error cases
description: relay/src/ws.rs:64-77 — The frame matching in the pre-auth phase catches Message::Text separately, then uses a catch-all for all other cases (binary, close, etc.). The catch-all sends INVALID_REQUEST, but it's not obvious why non-text frames are invalid at this point — it's the auth phase, so only text JSON-RPC requests make sense. A reader unfamiliar with WebSocket framing might wonder: why not just ignore binary/ping/pong as we do in the main loop? Fix: add a comment explaining that pre-auth phase must have JSON-RPC request in text form; other frame types are protocol violations at this point.
close_reason: P4 readability/comment items. Not blocking correctness. Deferred — file separately if prioritized.
